body_txt:
Being a Mook is a thankless job. Your boss has no respect for you, especially if you mess up , you live in constant fear of getting beaten up by the heroes, and you might just get offed anyway even if you do get your job done right. All in all, being a Mook or otherwise villainous subordinate is no fun, right? Well, not always. Sometimes, if the hero is particularly charming , friendly , or just plain fun to be around , the average minion may find themselves tempted to join in on the fun activities that the hero partakes in. Whether bobbing their heads or even singing along to a catchy musical number that isn’t by their boss , casually playing games with the enemy , and even asking for the hero's autograph! Sometimes this is just a momentary distraction for the heroes to defeat the henchmen or escape, but other times it’s a genuine attempt to be kind to their foes, even if it puts the heroes themselves a risk. Either way, the ensuing hilarity of seeing the villain’s minions partying with the hero, or otherwise being preoccupied from doing anything evil by having a good time is quite the sight to behold. Other baddies like the Bumbling Henchmen Duo , the Quirky Mini Boss Squad , and even The Dragon could end up dancing in a conga line with a particularly inclusive protagonist. How their Boss reacts can vary. Most of the time they’ll be absolutely incensed that their own men are daring to mingle with their arch nemesis and will swiftly attempt to punish anyone caught even briefly fraternizing with their adversary. And sometimes a simple Death Glare from the one in charge is enough to get the fearful henchmen to immediately drop any fun they’re having and continue their pursuit of their target.
Other times, particularly if it’s at the end of the story, the fun inspires the flunkies to switch sides , wanting to keep having the fun that their old leader never allowed them. On rare occasions, the positive vibes end up touching the main villain's black heart too, and they promptly give up their evil ambitions and join the good feelings club. If so, expect this trope to generally appear in more family-friendly and lighthearted works. The fun times don’t necessarily always have to be instigated by the hero. It can happen whenever any of the villain's henchmen get sidetracked by something enjoyable, like karaoke, a dance party, or chasing down the ever-enticing jingle of an ice cream truck. The boss is still likely to get upset at their underlings, even if the fun but distracting event has nothing to do with the good guys. Related somewhat to Villains Out Shopping , though in that instance they’re not necessarily doing anything fun, just engaging in mundane acts unrelated to villainy. Sub-Trope to Attention-Deficit Disciple and Actually Quite Catchy for musical numbers, dealing with a villain's henchmen getting caught up in having a good time, even at the risk of their boss's ire.
